prose:
Frontier-orbital calculation on water (H₂O) at the DFT level, tier 'standard' → B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fit RI), using PySCF via chemkit. Gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral closed-shell singlet). Geometry: user-supplied water.xyz, used as-is (single-point, not re-optimized at this level). SCF converged in 7 cycles. No warnings reported — clean run. Integrity check: status ok, trustworthy true, all checks passed.

Key results:
  • HOMO (index 4): −8.6037183 eV
  • LUMO (index 5): +0.33695037 eV
  • HOMO–LUMO gap: 8.9406687 eV  ← headline value
  • Total electronic energy: −76.462901 Hartree (−2080.6615 eV)

Koopmans descriptors: vertical IP = 8.6037 eV, vertical EA = −0.3370 eV, chemical hardness η = 4.4703 eV, electronegativity χ = 4.1334 eV, electrophilicity index ω = 1.9109 eV.

These are Koopmans-theorem orbital energies, not excitation energies. The total energy is a bare electronic energy meaningful only for same-method/same-basis comparisons. Provenance: computed (not experimental).
